Yet most existing benchmarks rely on simplistic, LLM-generated snippets or human-written solutions to code challenges and often restrict inputs and outputs to primitive types, failing to reflect the structure and dependencies of real-world projects. These simplifications limit their ability to measure practical generalizability. We present R2Eval1, a benchmark of 135 code reasoning problems drawn from ten widely used Python projects. Unlike prior work, R2Eval serializes compound and custom types, preserving real-world data complexity and enabling a more realistic assessment of LLMs.
title_zh: 在真实世界情境下评估大语言模型的代码推理能力
abstract_zh: 代码推理任务在评估大型语言模型（LLMs）方面变得日益重要。然而，现有的大多数基准测试依赖于简单的、由 LLM 生成的代码片段或人工编写的解题方案，且通常将输入和输出限制在基本类型上，无法反映真实项目中的结构与依赖关系。这些简化措施限制了其衡量实际泛化能力的能力。我们提出了 R2Eval1，一个包含 135 个代码推理问题的基准测试，这些问题均来自十个广泛使用的 Python 项目。与以往工作不同的是，R2Eval1 对复合类型和自定义类型进行了序列化处理，保留了真实世界数据的复杂性，从而能够更真实地评估 LLM 的表现。

title: Hyper Separation Logic (extended version)
authors: Trayan Gospodinov, Thibault Dardinier, Peter Müller
abstract: Many important functional and security properties--including non-interference, determinism, and generalized non-interference (GNI)--are hyperproperties, i.e., properties relating multiple executions of a program. Existing separation logics allow one to reason about specific classes of hyperproperties, e.g., $\forall\forall$-hyperproperties such as non-interference and $\exists\exists$-properties such as non-determinism. However, they do not support quantifier alternation, which is for instance needed to express GNI. The only existing logic that can reason about such properties is Hyper Hoare Logic, but it does not support heap-manipulating programs and, thus, is not applicable to common imperative programs. This paper introduces Hyper Separation Logic (HSL), the first program logic that supports modular reasoning about hyperproperties with arbitrary quantifier alternation over programs that manipulate the heap. HSL generalizes Hyper Hoare Logic with a novel hyper separating conjunction that lifts the standard separating conjunction to sets of states, enabling a generalized frame rule for hyperproperties. We prove HSL sound in Isabelle/HOL and demonstrate its expressiveness for hyperproperties that lie beyond the reach of existing separation logics.
title_zh: 超分离逻辑（扩展版）
abstract_zh: 许多重要的功能和安全属性——包括非干扰性、确定性以及广义非干扰性（GNI）——都是超性质（hyperproperties），即涉及程序多个执行实例之间关系的性质。现有的分离逻辑仅能对特定类别的超性质进行推理，例如 $\forall\forall$-型超性质（如非干扰性）和 $\exists\exists$-型性质（如非确定性）。然而，它们不支持量词交替，而这一点正是表达 GNI 所必需的。目前唯一能够处理此类性质的逻辑是超霍尔逻辑（Hyper Hoare Logic），但它不支持堆内存操作的程序，因此无法应用于常见的命令式程序。本文提出了超分离逻辑（Hyper Separation Logic, HSL），这是首个支持对具有任意量词交替的超性质进行模块化推理的程序逻辑，适用于操纵堆内存的程序。HSL 在 Hyper Hoare Logic 的基础上进行了扩展，引入了一种新颖的“超分离合取”（hyper separating conjunction），将标准的分离合取推广到状态集合上，从而为超性质提供了广义的框架规则。我们已在 Isabelle/HOL 中证明了 HSL 的正确性，并展示了其在表达超越现有分离逻辑能力范围的超性质方面的强大表达力。

title: CodeSpecBench: Benchmarking LLMs for Executable Behavioral Specification Generation
authors: Zaoyu Chen, Jianbo Dai, Boyu Zhu, Jingdong Wang, Huiming Wang, Xin Xu, Haoyang Yuan, Zhijiang Guo, Xiao-Ming Wu
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) can generate code from natural language, but the extent to which they capture intended program behavior remains unclear. Executable behavioral specifications, defined via preconditions and postconditions, provide a concrete means to assess such understanding. However, existing work on specification generation is constrained in evaluation methodology, task settings, and specification expressiveness. We introduce CodeSpecBench, a benchmark for executable behavioral specification generation under an execution-based evaluation protocol. CodeSpecBench supports both function-level and repository-level tasks and encodes specifications as executable Python functions. Constructed from diverse real-world codebases, it enables a realistic assessment of both correctness (accepting valid behaviors) and completeness (rejecting invalid behaviors). Evaluating 15 state-of-the-art LLMs on CodeSpecBench, we observe a sharp performance degradation on repository-level tasks, where the best model attains only a 20.2% pass rate. We further find that specification generation is substantially more challenging than code generation, indicating that strong coding performance does not necessarily reflect deep understanding of intended program semantics. Our data and code are available at https://github.com/SparksofAGI/CodeSpecBench.
title_zh: CodeSpecBench：用于可执行行为规范生成的大型语言模型基准测试
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）能够从自然语言生成代码，但它们在多大程度上捕捉了程序的预期行为仍不明确。通过前置条件和后置条件定义的可执行行为规范，为评估这种理解能力提供了一种具体方法。然而，现有的规范生成研究在评估方法、任务设置和规范表达能力方面均存在局限。为此，我们提出了 CodeSpecBench，一个基于执行评估协议的可执行行为规范生成基准。CodeSpecBench 支持函数级和仓库级两种任务，并将规范编码为可执行的 Python 函数。该基准数据集源自多样化的实际代码库，能够对正确性（接受有效行为）和完整性（拒绝无效行为）进行真实可靠的评估。我们在 CodeSpecBench 上对 15 个最先进的 LLM 进行评估，发现模型在仓库级任务上的性能急剧下降，表现最好的模型仅达到 20.2% 的通过率。此外，我们还发现规范生成任务远比代码生成更具挑战性，表明强大的编码能力并不一定意味着对程序语义意图的深层理解。我们的数据与代码已公开于 https://github.com/SparksofAGI/CodeSpecBench。

title: Frontier-Eng: Benchmarking Self-Evolving Agents on Real-World Engineering Tasks with Generative Optimization
authors: Yizhe Chi, Deyao Hong, Dapeng Jiang, Tianwei Luo, Kaisen Yang, Boshi Zhang, Zhe Cao, Xiaoyan Fan, Bingxiang He, Han Hao, Weiyang Jin, Dianqiao Lei, Qingle Liu, Houde Qian, Bowen Wang, Situ Wang, Youjie Zheng, Yifan Zhou, Calvin Xiao, Eren Cai, Qinhuai Na
abstract: Current LLM agent benchmarks, which predominantly focus on binary pass/fail tasks such as code generation or search-based question answering, often neglect the value of real-world engineering that is often captured through the iterative optimization of feasible designs. To this end, we introduce Frontier-Eng, a human-verified benchmark for generative optimization -- an iterative propose-execute-evaluate loop in which an agent generates candidate artifacts, receives executable verifier feedback, and revises them under a fixed interaction budget -- spanning $47$ tasks across five broad engineering categories. Unlike previous suites, Frontier-Eng tasks are grounded in industrial-grade simulators and verifiers that provide continuous reward signals and enforce hard feasibility constraints under constrained budgets. We evaluate eight frontier language models using representative search frameworks, finding that while Claude 4.6 Opus achieves the most robust performance, the benchmark remains challenging for all models. Our analysis suggests a dual power-law decay in improvement frequency ($\sim$ 1/iteration) and magnitude ($\sim$ 1/improvement count). We further show that although width improves parallelism and diversity, depth remains crucial for hard-won improvements under a fixed budget. Frontier-Eng establishes a new standard for assessing the capacity of AI agents to integrate domain knowledge with executable feedback to solve complex, open-ended engineering problems.
title_zh: 前沿工程：基于生成优化的现实世界工程任务中自演化智能体的基准测试
abstract_zh: 当前的大规模语言模型（LLM）代理评估基准主要聚焦于二元通过/失败的任务，如代码生成或基于搜索的问答任务，往往忽视了真实工程实践中通过迭代优化可行设计方案所体现的价值。为此，我们提出了 Frontier-Eng——一个经过人工验证的生成式优化基准，该基准采用“提出-执行-评估”的迭代循环模式：智能体生成候选方案，接收可执行的验证反馈，并在固定交互预算内进行修正，涵盖五大工程领域共47项任务。与以往基准不同，Frontier-Eng 的任务基于工业级仿真器和验证工具构建，能够提供连续的奖励信号，并在资源受限的条件下施加严格的可行性约束。我们使用代表性搜索框架对八种前沿语言模型进行了评估，发现尽管 Claude 4.6 Opus 表现最为稳健，但该基准对所有模型而言仍极具挑战性。我们的分析表明，性能提升的频率呈现近似于 1/迭代次数的双幂律衰减，而每次改进的幅度则近似于 1/改进次数的衰减。此外，我们还发现虽然增加宽度有助于提升并行性和多样性，但在固定预算下，深度仍是实现关键突破性改进的核心因素。Frontier-Eng 为评估人工智能代理在解决复杂、开放式的工程问题时，融合领域知识与可执行反馈的能力，树立了新的标准。

title: Structural Anchors and Reasoning Fragility:Understanding CoT Robustness in LLM4Code
authors: Yang Liu, Da Song, Armstrong Foundjem, Heng Li, Foutse Khomh
abstract: Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting is widely used to elicit explicit reasoning from large language models for code (LLM4Code). However, its impact on robustness and the stability of reasoning trajectories under realistic input perturbations remains poorly understood. Prior work has largely evaluated CoT through final correctness, leaving a critical gap in understanding how CoT reshapes internal uncertainty dynamics and why it sometimes harms rather than helps code generation. We suggest that CoT is not uniformly beneficial; instead, its robustness depends on whether perturbations destabilize structurally sensitive commitment points along the reasoning-to-code trajectory. We conduct a controlled, large-scale empirical study of CoT across six models and two code benchmarks (MHPP and BigCodeBench), subjecting task docstrings to systematic character-, word-, and sentence-level perturbations. We instrument full generation traces with token-level uncertainty and define three novel structural anchors: reasoning-code transition, symbolic commitment, and algorithmic articulation. Findings: (1) CoT does not yield uniform performance or robustness gains: its benefits are contingent on model family, task structure, and prompt explicitness. (2) CoT and No-CoT exhibit distinct robustness profiles, with different perturbation families triggering different failure modes. (3) We identify three recurrent trajectory deformations--Lengthening, Branching, and Simplification--that systematically emerge when perturbations interact with structural anchors and explain failure patterns. (4) Early-stage uncertainty serves as a reliable diagnostic signal for localizing where trajectory instability begins around sensitive anchors. These results provide a unified explanation for CoT's mixed performance and suggest design principles for building more robust reasoning-based code generators.
title_zh: 结构化锚点与推理脆弱性：理解 LLM4Code 中的思维链鲁棒性
abstract_zh:
思维链（Chain-of-Thought, CoT）提示在大型语言模型代码生成（LLM4Code）中被广泛使用，旨在激发模型显式推理过程。然而，其在面对现实输入扰动时对模型鲁棒性及推理轨迹稳定性的影响仍缺乏深入理解。以往研究主要依赖最终正确率来评估CoT效果，这导致了一个关键空白：未能揭示CoT如何重塑模型内部的不确定性动态，以及为何有时反而会损害代码生成质量。我们提出，CoT并非始终有益；其鲁棒性取决于输入扰动是否破坏了从推理到代码生成路径中的结构性敏感节点。为此，我们开展了一项受控的大规模实证研究，涵盖六种模型和两个代码基准（MHPP与BigCodeBench），对任务文档字符串施加系统性的字符级、词级和句子级扰动。我们在完整生成过程中引入token级别的不确定性度量，并定义了三个新型结构锚点：推理-代码转换点、符号承诺点、算法表达点。

研究发现如下：  
(1) CoT并未带来一致的性能或鲁棒性提升：其优势取决于模型家族、任务结构以及提示的明确程度；  
(2) CoT与无CoT模式表现出截然不同的鲁棒性特征，不同类型的扰动会引发不同的失效模式；  
(3) 我们识别出三种反复出现的轨迹畸变现象——延长（Lengthening）、分支（Branching）与简化（Simplification），这些现象在扰动与结构锚点相互作用时系统性地出现，并可解释各类失败模式；  
(4) 早期阶段的不确定性水平可作为可靠的诊断信号，用于定位轨迹不稳定性在敏感锚点附近开始的具体位置。

上述结果为CoT表现参差不齐的现象提供了统一解释，并提出了构建更鲁棒的基于推理的代码生成器的设计原则。

title: Beyond Scores: Diagnostic LLM Evaluation via Fine-Grained Abilities
authors: Xu Zhang, Xudong Gong, Jiacheng Qin, Qiang Wang, JiaQi Liao, Zhe Wang, Dawei Feng, Bo Ding
abstract: Current evaluations of large language models aggregate performance across diverse tasks into single scores. This obscures fine-grained ability variation, limiting targeted model improvement and ability-guided selection for specific tasks. Motivated by this gap, we propose a cognitive diagnostic framework that estimates model abilities across multiple fine-grained dimensions. For mathematics, we construct a 35-dimensional ability taxonomy grounded in cognitive theory and domain knowledge. The framework employs multidimensional Item Response Theory with an item-ability association matrix to estimate fine-grained ability levels, which in turn enable prediction of performance on unseen items (questions of benchmark). Evaluated on 41 models, our approach demonstrates strong criterion validity, consistent ability estimates across benchmarks, and accurate prediction of unseen items with AUC ranging from 0.80 to 0.89 within benchmarks and from 0.77 to 0.86 across benchmarks, substantially exceeding trivial baselines. The framework generalizes across scientific domains, producing consistent diagnostic performance in physics (27 dimensions), chemistry (58 dimensions), and computer science (12 dimensions). This work establishes a principled framework for fine-grained assessment of abilities, with potential applications in targeted training, ability-guided model selection, and ability-aware benchmark design.
title_zh: 超越分数：通过细粒度能力进行诊断性大语言模型评估
abstract_zh: 当前对大型语言模型的评估通常将不同任务上的表现汇总为单一分数，这掩盖了模型在细粒度能力维度上的差异，限制了针对性的模型优化以及针对特定任务的能力导向选择。为弥补这一不足，我们提出了一种认知诊断框架，用于估计模型在多个细粒度维度上的能力水平。针对数学领域，我们基于认知理论和领域知识构建了一个包含35个维度的能力分类体系。该框架采用多维项目反应理论，并结合项目-能力关联矩阵，以估算细粒度的能力水平，进而实现对未见项目（基准测试中的题目）表现的预测。在41个模型上的评估表明，该方法具有较强的效标效度，在不同基准间保持一致的能力估计，并能准确预测未见项目，其AUC值在单个基准内为0.80至0.89，在跨基准情况下为0.77至0.86，显著优于简单的基线方法。该框架可推广至多个科学领域，在物理（27维）、化学（58维）和计算机科学（12维）中均表现出一致的诊断性能。本研究建立了一个系统化的细粒度能力评估框架，具有在针对性训练、能力导向的模型选择以及能力感知的基准设计等方面的广泛应用前景。

title: Knowledge Is Not Static: Order-Aware Hypergraph RAG for Language Models
authors: Keshu Wu, Chenchen Kuai, Zihao Li, Jiwan Jiang, Shiyu Shen, Shian Wang, Chan-Wei Hu, Zhengzhong Tu, Yang Zhou
abstract: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) enhances large language models by grounding outputs in retrieved knowledge. However, existing RAG methods including graph- and hypergraph-based approaches treat retrieved evidence as an unordered set, implicitly assuming permutation invariance. This assumption is misaligned with many real-world reasoning tasks, where outcomes depend not only on which interactions occur, but also on the order in which they unfold. We propose Order-Aware Knowledge Hypergraph RAG (OKH-RAG), which treats order as a first-class structural property. OKH-RAG represents knowledge as higher-order interactions within a hypergraph augmented with precedence structure, and reformulates retrieval as sequence inference over hyperedges. Instead of selecting independent facts, it recovers coherent interaction trajectories that reflect underlying reasoning processes. A learned transition model infers precedence directly from data without requiring explicit temporal supervision. We evaluate OKH-RAG on order-sensitive question answering and explanation tasks, including tropical cyclone and port operation scenarios. OKH-RAG consistently outperforms permutation-invariant baselines, and ablations show that these gains arise specifically from modeling interaction order. These results highlight a key limitation of set-based retrieval: effective reasoning requires not only retrieving relevant evidence, but organizing it into structured sequences.
title_zh: 知识并非静态：面向顺序的超图RAG语言模型
abstract_zh: 检索增强生成（RAG）通过将模型输出与检索到的知识相结合，提升了大型语言模型的能力。然而，现有的RAG方法，包括基于图和超图的方法，都将检索到的证据视为无序集合，隐含地假设了排列不变性。这一假设与许多现实世界中的推理任务不一致，因为在这些任务中，结果不仅取决于哪些交互发生，还取决于它们发生的顺序。为此，我们提出了**有序感知知识超图RAG（OKH-RAG）**，将顺序视为一种首要的结构属性。OKH-RAG将知识表示为带有优先级结构的超图中的高阶交互，并将检索重新定义为对超边序列的推断过程。与独立选择事实不同，它能够恢复出反映底层推理过程的连贯交互轨迹。一个学习得到的转移模型可以直接从数据中推断出先后顺序，而无需显式的时序标注。我们在对顺序敏感的问题回答和解释任务上评估了OKH-RAG，包括热带气旋和港口运营等场景。实验结果表明，OKH-RAG始终优于排列不变的基线方法，消融实验进一步证明这些性能提升正是源于对交互顺序的建模。这些结果揭示了一个关键局限：基于集合的检索方法存在不足——有效的推理不仅需要检索相关证据，还需要将其组织成有结构的序列。

title: RePAIR: Interactive Machine Unlearning through Prompt-Aware Model Repair
authors: Jagadeesh Rachapudi, Pranav Singh, Ritali Vatsi, Praful Hambarde, Amit Shukla
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) inherently absorb harmful knowledge, misinformation, and personal data during pretraining on large-scale web corpora, with no native mechanism for selective removal. While machine unlearning offers a principled solution, existing approaches are provider-centric, requiring retraining pipelines, curated retain datasets, and direct intervention by model service providers (MSPs), thereby excluding end users from controlling their own data. We introduce Interactive Machine Unlearning (IMU), a new paradigm in which users can instruct LLMs to forget targeted knowledge through natural language at inference time. To realize IMU, we propose RePAIR, a prompt-aware model repair framework comprising (i) a watchdog model for unlearning intent detection, (ii) a surgeon model for generating repair procedures, and (iii) a patient model whose parameters are updated autonomously. At the core of RePAIR, we develop Steering Through Activation Manipulation with PseudoInverse (STAMP), a training-free, single-sample unlearning method that redirects MLP activations toward a refusal subspace via closed-form pseudoinverse updates. Its low-rank variant reduces computational complexity from O(d^3) to O(r^3 + r^2 * d), enabling efficient on-device unlearning with up to ~3x speedup over training-based baselines. Extensive experiments across harmful knowledge suppression, misinformation correction, and personal data erasure demonstrate that RePAIR achieves near-zero forget scores (Acc_f = 0.00, F-RL = 0.00) while preserving model utility (Acc_r up to 84.47, R-RL up to 0.88), outperforming six state-of-the-art baselines. These results establish RePAIR as an effective and practical framework for user-driven model editing, advancing transparent and on-device control over learned knowledge, with potential extensions to multimodal foundation models.
title_zh: RePAIR：通过提示感知模型修复实现交互式机器遗忘
abstract_zh:
大型语言模型（LLMs）在大规模网络语料上进行预训练时，会不可避免地吸收有害知识、错误信息和个人数据，且缺乏原生的针对性删除机制。尽管机器遗忘（machine unlearning）提供了一种原则性的解决方案，但现有方法多依赖于服务提供商，需要重新训练流程、精心设计的保留数据集，并由模型服务提供商（MSPs）直接介入，导致终端用户无法自主控制自身数据。为此，我们提出一种全新的范式——交互式机器遗忘（Interactive Machine Unlearning, IMU），使用户能够在推理阶段通过自然语言指令，引导大模型主动“遗忘”特定知识。

为实现IMU，我们提出了RePAIR——一种提示感知的模型修复框架，包含三个核心组件：(i) 监视器模型（watchdog model），用于检测用户的遗忘意图；(ii) 外科医生模型（surgeon model），负责生成具体的修复操作；(iii) 患者模型（patient model），其参数可自主更新。RePAIR的核心是STAMP（Steering Through Activation Manipulation with PseudoInverse）——一种无需训练、仅需单样本即可完成遗忘的高效方法。该方法通过闭式伪逆更新，将MLP层的激活值导向“拒绝子空间”，从而实现对目标知识的有效抑制。其低秩变体将计算复杂度从O(d³)降低至O(r³ + r²·d)，显著提升了效率，在设备端实现遗忘的速度相比基于训练的方法最高提升约3倍。

在有害知识抑制、虚假信息纠正及个人数据擦除等任务上的大量实验表明，RePAIR实现了近乎零遗忘得分（Acc_f = 0.00，F-RL = 0.00），同时有效保持了模型性能（Acc_r高达84.47，R-RL达0.88），优于六种当前最先进的基线方法。这些结果证明，RePAIR是一种高效且实用的用户驱动模型编辑框架，推动了对所学知识的透明化与设备端自主控制，未来有望拓展至多模态基础模型领域。

title: Short Version of VERIFAI2026 Paper -- Learning Infused Formal Reasoning: Contract Synthesis, Artefact Reuse and Semantic Foundations
authors: Arshad Beg, Diarmuid O'Donoghue, Rosemary Monahan
abstract: Artificial intelligence systems have achieved remarkable capability in natural language processing, perception and decision-making tasks. However, their behaviour often remains opaque and difficult to verify, limiting their applicability in safety-critical systems. Formal methods provide mathematically rigorous mechanisms for specifying and verifying system behaviour, yet the creation and maintenance of formal specifications remains labour intensive and difficult to scale. This paper outlines a research vision called Learning-Infused Formal Reasoning (LIFR), which integrates machine learning techniques with formal verification workflows. The framework focuses on three complementary research directions: automated contract synthesis from natural language requirements, semantic reuse of verification artifacts using graph matching and learning-based embeddings, and mathematically grounded semantic foundations based on the Unifying Theories of Programming (UTP) and the Theory of Institutions. Together these research threads aim to transform verification from isolated correctness proofs into a cumulative knowledge-driven process where specifications, contracts and proofs can be synthesised, aligned and reused across systems.
title_zh: VERIFAI2026论文简版——融合学习的正式推理：合约生成、资产复用与语义基础
abstract_zh: 人工智能系统在自然语言处理、感知和决策任务方面已取得显著进展。然而，其行为往往缺乏透明性且难以验证，这限制了它们在安全关键系统中的应用。形式化方法提供了数学上严格的方法来规范和验证系统行为，但形式规范的创建与维护仍十分耗时，且难以扩展。本文提出了一种名为“学习融合形式推理”（Learning-Infused Formal Reasoning, LIFR）的研究愿景，将机器学习技术与形式化验证流程相结合。该框架聚焦于三个互补的研究方向：从自然语言需求自动生成契约、利用图匹配与基于学习的嵌入实现验证成果的语义复用，以及基于统一程序理论（UTP）和制度论（Theory of Institutions）的数学基础支撑的语义体系。这三个研究方向共同致力于将验证从孤立的正确性证明转变为一种累积性的知识驱动过程，使规范、契约与证明能够在不同系统间实现合成、对齐与复用。

title: EPAC: The Last Dance
authors: Filippo Mantovani, Fabio Banchelli, Pablo Vizcaino, Roger Ferrer, Oscar Palomar, Francesco Minervini, Jesus Labarta, Mauro Olivieri, Sebastiano Pomata, Pedro Marcuello, Jordi Cortina, Alberto Moreno, Josep Sans, Roger Espasa, Vassilis Papaefstathiou, Nikolaos Dimou, Georgios Ieronymakis, Antonis Psathakis, Michalis Giaourtas, Iasonas Mastorakis, Manolis Marazakis, Eric Guthmuller, Andrea Bocco, Jérôme Fereyre, César Fuguet, Mate Kovač, Mario Kovač, Luka Mrković, Josip Ramljak, Luca Bertaccini, Tim Fischer, Frank K. Gurkaynak, Paul Scheffler, Luca Benini, Bhavishya Goel, Madhavan Manivannan, Tiago Rocha, Nuno Neves, Jens Krüger
abstract: This paper presents EPAC, a RISC-V-based accelerator chip developed within the European Processor Initiative (EPI) as part of a multi-year, multi-partner effort to build a European HPC processor ecosystem. EPAC is implemented in GlobalFoundries 22FDX (GF22FDX) technology, covers an area of 27 sq mm with approximately 0.3 billion transistors, and integrates three distinct RISC-V compute tiles targeting different workload classes: VEC, a vector processing tile for double-precision HPC workloads; STX, a many-core tile optimized for stencil and machine learning computations; and VRP, a variable-precision tile for iterative numerical solvers requiring extended floating-point formats. All tiles are connected through a Coherent Hub Interface (CHI) based network-on-chip with a distributed L2 cache system and communicate with external memory via a SerDes link. The chip was taped out in GF22FDX technology and successfully brought up, with all major IP blocks validated. This paper describes the architecture of each tile and the uncore infrastructure, the integration and physical implementation process, and the board-level bring-up activities. It also reflects on the engineering and coordination lessons learned from a full chip design effort distributed across academic and industrial partners in Europe.
title_zh: EPAC：最后一舞
abstract_zh: 本文介绍了EPAC，这是一款基于RISC-V架构的加速器芯片，由欧洲处理器计划（EPI）在一项多年、多方合作的项目中开发，旨在构建欧洲高性能计算（HPC）处理器生态系统。EPAC采用格罗方德22FDX（GF22FDX）工艺实现，芯片面积为27平方毫米，包含约30亿个晶体管。该芯片集成了三个针对不同工作负载类别的RISC-V计算单元：VEC，用于双精度HPC计算的向量处理单元；STX，专为交错计算和机器学习优化的多核单元；以及VRP，适用于需要扩展浮点格式的迭代数值求解器的可变精度单元。所有计算单元通过基于一致性枢纽接口（CHI）的片上网络连接，并配备分布式L2缓存系统，同时通过SerDes链路与外部内存通信。该芯片已在GF22FDX工艺下完成流片并成功启动，所有主要IP模块均已验证。本文详细描述了各计算单元及非核心基础设施的架构设计、集成与物理实现流程，以及板级启动的相关工作。此外，文章还总结了在欧洲多个学术与工业合作伙伴共同参与的全芯片设计过程中所获得的工程实践与协作经验教训。

title: Latent-Condensed Transformer for Efficient Long Context Modeling
authors: Zeng You, Yaofo Chen, Qiuwu Chen, Ying Sun, Shuhai Zhang, Yingjian Li, Yaowei Wang, Mingkui Tan
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) face significant challenges in processing long contexts due to the linear growth of the key-value (KV) cache and quadratic complexity of self-attention. Existing approaches address these bottlenecks separately: Multi-head Latent Attention (MLA) reduces the KV cache by projecting tokens into a low-dimensional latent space, while sparse attention reduces computation. However, sparse methods cannot operate natively on MLA's compressed latent structure, missing opportunities for joint optimization. In this paper, we propose Latent-Condensed Attention (LCA), which directly condenses context within MLA's latent space, where the representation is disentangled into semantic latent vectors and positional keys. LCA separately aggregates semantic vectors via query-aware pooling and preserves positional keys via anchor selection. This approach jointly reduces both computational cost and KV cache without adding parameters. Beyond MLA, LCA's design is architecture-agnostic and readily extends to other attention mechanisms such as GQA. Theoretically, we prove a length-independent error bound. Experiments show LCA achieves up to 2.5$\times$ prefilling speedup and 90% KV cache reduction at 128K context while maintaining competitive performance.
title_zh: 用于高效长上下文建模的潜在凝聚Transformer
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）在处理长上下文时面临重大挑战，主要源于键值（KV）缓存的线性增长以及自注意力机制的二次计算复杂度。现有的解决方案通常分别应对这些瓶颈：多头潜在注意力（MLA）通过将token投影到低维潜在空间来减少KV缓存，而稀疏注意力则旨在降低计算开销。然而，稀疏方法无法原生作用于MLA所生成的压缩潜在结构，因而错失了联合优化的机会。本文提出了一种新的“潜在浓缩注意力”（Latent-Condensed Attention, LCA），直接在MLA的潜在空间内对上下文进行浓缩。该方法将表示解耦为语义潜在向量和位置键，并通过查询感知池化分别聚合语义向量，同时通过锚点选择保留位置键。这一设计在不增加参数的前提下，同时降低了计算成本与KV缓存占用。此外，LCA的设计具有架构无关性，可轻松扩展至其他注意力机制（如分组查询注意力GQA）。理论上，我们证明了LCA具有与序列长度无关的误差界。实验结果表明，在128K上下文长度下，LCA实现了最高达2.5倍的预填充速度提升，并减少了90%的KV缓存，同时保持了具有竞争力的性能表现。

title: Heuristic Classification of Thoughts Prompting (HCoT): Integrating Expert System Heuristics for Structured Reasoning into Large Language Models
authors: Lei Lin, Jizhao Zhu, Yong Liu, Donghong Sun, Hongbo He, Yihua Du
abstract: This paper addresses two limitations of large language models (LLMs) in solving complex problems: (1) their reasoning processes exhibit Bayesian-like stochastic generation, where each token is sampled from a context-dependent probability distribution, leading to inherently random decision trajectories rather than deterministic planning; (2) the reasoning and decision-making mechanisms are statically decoupled, meaning dynamically retrieved domain knowledge fails to dynamically adjust the underlying reasoning strategy. These dual deficiencies result in initial decisions lacking strategic anchoring and reasoning chains often failing to converge on correct solutions, as stochastic generation lacks mechanisms for trajectory correction or knowledge-guided optimization during sequential reasoning. To resolve these issues, we propose a problem-solving method integrated into the LLM's generation process to guide reasoning. This method, compatible with numerous LLMs and featuring reusable solutions, is grounded in a novel Heuristic-Classification-of-Thoughts prompting schema (HCoT). HCoT synergizes the LLM's reasoning ability with a structured problem space via a heuristic classification model that controls the reasoning process and provides reusable abstract solutions. Evaluated on two complex inductive reasoning tasks with ill-defined search spaces, HCoT outperforms existing approaches (e.g., Tree-of-Thoughts and Chain-of-Thoughts prompting) in performance. On the well-structured 24 Game task, HCoT demonstrates significantly higher token efficiency compared to the state-of-the-art Tree-of-Thoughts-Breadth-First-Search. In terms of both accuracy and token usage, HCoT achieves a Pareto frontier balance, offering a strong trade-off between performance and computational cost.
title_zh: 启发式思维提示分类（HCoT）：将专家系统启发式方法融入大型语言模型以实现结构化推理
abstract_zh: 本文针对大型语言模型（LLMs）在解决复杂问题时存在的两大局限性展开研究：（1）其推理过程表现出类似贝叶斯的随机生成特性，即每个标记（token）均从依赖上下文的概率分布中采样生成，导致决策路径本质上是随机的，而非确定性的规划；（2）推理与决策机制存在静态解耦，动态检索到的领域知识无法动态调整底层的推理策略。这两种缺陷使得初始决策缺乏战略锚定，推理链条往往难以收敛至正确答案，因为随机生成过程缺乏在序列推理过程中进行轨迹修正或基于知识引导优化的机制。为解决上述问题，我们提出一种嵌入于LLM生成过程中的问题求解方法，用以引导推理。该方法兼容多种LLM，并具备可复用解决方案的特点，其基础是一种新颖的“思维启发式分类”提示框架（HCoT）。HCoT通过一个启发式分类模型，将LLM的推理能力与结构化的问题空间相结合，从而控制推理流程并提供可复用的抽象解法。在两个搜索空间定义不明确的复杂归纳推理任务上进行评估，HCoT的表现优于现有方法（如思维树和思维链提示）。在结构清晰的“24点游戏”任务中，HCoT相较于最先进的“思维树-广度优先搜索”方法展现出显著更高的令牌效率。在准确率与令牌使用量两方面，HCoT实现了帕累托最优平衡，提供了性能与计算成本之间的优异权衡。

title: An Engineering Journey Training Large Language Models at Scale on Alps: The Apertus Experience
authors: Jonathan Coles, Stefano Schuppli, Lukas Drescher, Fawzi Roberto Mohamed, Elia Palme, Henrique Mendonça, Miguel Gila, Mark Klein, Maxime Martinasso, Joost VandeVondele, Torsten Hoefler, Thomas Schulthess, Josh Romero, Igor Gorodetsky, Ryan Hankins, Isa Wazirzada, Martin Jaggi, Antoine Bosselut, Imanol Schlag, Antoni-Joan Solergibert i Llaquet, Alejandro Hernández Cano, Theofilos Ioannis Manitaras, Nicholas John Browning
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have surged as a transformative technology for science and society, prompting governments worldwide to pursue sovereign AI capabilities that ensure data compliance and cultural representation. However, the associated capital costs and engineering complexity required to train these models have largely restricted such capabilities to the private sector, leaving a significant gap for public institutions. This paper details the engineering journey behind training \textit{Apertus}, a fully open multilingual foundation model, on the \textit{Alps} supercomputer. Representing a first-of-its-kind achievement for academia at the 70B parameter scale, we successfully deployed a massive pre-training campaign on one of Europe's largest systems for open science, powered by NVIDIA GH200 Grace Hopper Superchips. We detail the challenges encountered in readying HPC infrastructure for training AI models, from overcoming storage bottlenecks to stabilizing large-scale interconnects, and the lessons learned in transforming a supercomputer into a resilient software-defined Machine Learning Platform. Finally, we discuss the post-training requirements and evolution of our Machine Learning platform, outlining how this initial release lays the groundwork for a sustained, iterative operational capability, in particular for fine tuning foundation models, that extends well beyond a single model training run.
title_zh: 在阿尔卑斯山大规模训练大语言模型的工程之旅：阿佩图斯（Apertus）经验分享
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）作为一项变革性技术，正在深刻影响科学与社会的发展，促使全球各国政府致力于构建自主可控的人工智能能力，以确保数据合规性并体现文化多样性。然而，训练这些模型所需的巨额资本投入和复杂的工程挑战，使得此类能力主要局限于私营部门，公共机构因此面临显著的能力缺口。本文详细阐述了在欧洲最大的开放科学超算系统——\textit{Alps}超级计算机上，训练完全开源的多语言基础模型 \textit{Apertus} 的工程历程。作为学术界首次在700亿参数规模实现的突破性成果，我们成功在欧洲最大规模的高性能计算系统之一上部署了一次大规模预训练任务，该系统由NVIDIA GH200 Grace Hopper超级芯片驱动。文中深入分析了为AI模型训练准备高性能计算基础设施所面临的诸多挑战，包括克服存储瓶颈、稳定大规模互连网络等，并总结了将传统超算转型为稳健的软件定义机器学习平台的关键经验。最后，我们探讨了模型训练后的持续需求与平台演进路径，阐明此次发布不仅是一次性的模型训练成果，更奠定了一个可持续、迭代式运行能力的基础，尤其为后续的基础模型微调等任务提供了长期支持，其价值远超单次训练周期。

title: Design automation and space-time reduction for surface-code logical operations using a SAT-based EDA kernel compatible with general encodings
authors: Wang Liao, Rei Tokami, Yasunari Suzuki
abstract: Fault-tolerant quantum computers (FTQCs) based on surface codes and lattice surgery have been widely studied, and there is strong demand for a framework that can identify logical operations with low space-time cost, verify their functionality and fault tolerance, and demonstrate their optimality within a given search space, much like electronic design automation (EDA) in classical circuit design. In this paper, we propose KOVAL-Q, an EDA kernel that verifies and optimizes surface-code logical operations by formulating them as a satisfiability (SAT) problem. Compared with existing SAT-based frameworks such as LaSsynth, our method can handle logical qubits with more flexible surface-code encodings, both as target configurations and as intermediate states. This extension enables the optimization of advanced layouts, such as fast blocks, and broadens the search space for logical operations. We demonstrate that KOVAL-Q can determine the minimum execution time of fundamental logical operations in given spatial layouts, such as $d$-cycle logical CNOTs and $2d$-cycle patch rotations. Their use reduces the execution time of widely studied FTQC applications by about 10% under a simplified scheduling model. KOVAL-Q consists of three subkernels corresponding to different types of constraints, which facilitates its integration as a submodule into scalable heuristic frameworks. Thus, our proposal provides an essential framework for optimizing and validating core FTQC subroutines.
title_zh: 基于SAT的EDA内核的表面码逻辑操作设计自动化与时空优化，兼容通用编码
abstract_zh: 基于表面码和晶格手术的容错量子计算机（FTQCs）已得到广泛研究，人们迫切需要一种能够识别具有低时空成本的逻辑操作、验证其功能与容错性，并在给定搜索空间内证明其最优性的框架，类似于经典电路设计中的电子设计自动化（EDA）。本文提出KOVAL-Q，一个用于验证和优化表面码逻辑操作的EDA核心模块，将逻辑操作建模为可满足性（SAT）问题。与现有的基于SAT的框架（如LaSsynth）相比，我们的方法能够处理更灵活的表面码编码方式，既可用于目标配置，也可作为中间状态。这一扩展使得高级布局（如快速块）的优化成为可能，并显著拓宽了逻辑操作的搜索空间。我们证明，KOVAL-Q能够在给定的空间布局中确定基本逻辑操作的最小执行时间，例如$d$-周期逻辑CNOT门和$2d$-周期块旋转。在简化的调度模型下，这些优化可使广泛研究的FTQC应用的执行时间减少约10%。KOVAL-Q由三个对应不同约束类型的子核组成，便于其作为模块集成到可扩展的启发式框架中。因此，本研究提供了一个优化和验证FTQC核心子程序的关键框架。

title: Personalizing LLM-Based Conversational Programming Assistants
authors: Jonan Richards
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have shown much promise in powering a variety of software engineering (SE) tools. Offering natural language as an intuitive interaction mechanism, LLMs have recently been employed as conversational ``programming assistants'' capable of supporting several SE activities simultaneously. As with any SE tool, it is crucial that these assistants effectively meet developers' needs. Recent studies have shown addressing this challenge is complicated by the variety in developers' needs, and the ambiguous and unbounded nature of conversational interaction. This paper discusses our current and future work towards characterizing how diversity in cognition and organizational context impacts developers' needs, and exploring personalization as a means of improving the inclusivity of LLM-based conversational programming assistants.
title_zh: 个性化基于大语言模型的对话式编程助手
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）在推动各类软件工程（SE）工具发展方面展现出巨大潜力。通过提供自然语言这一直观的交互方式，LLMs 近期被用作对话式“编程助手”，能够同时支持多种软件工程活动。与任何软件工程工具一样，这些助手能否有效满足开发者的实际需求至关重要。近期研究表明，这一挑战的复杂性源于开发者需求的多样性，以及对话交互中固有的模糊性和无限扩展性。本文探讨了我们当前及未来的研究工作，旨在揭示认知差异和组织背景多样性如何影响开发者的实际需求，并探索个性化机制作为提升基于 LLM 的对话式编程助手包容性的有效途径。

title: CoDe-R: Refining Decompiler Output with LLMs via Rationale Guidance and Adaptive Inference
authors: Qiang Zhang, Zhongnian Li
abstract: Binary decompilation is a critical reverse engineering task aimed at reconstructing high-level source code from stripped executables. Although Large Language Models (LLMs) have recently shown promise, they often suffer from "logical hallucinations" and "semantic misalignment" due to the irreversible semantic loss during compilation, resulting in generated code that fails to re-execute. In this study, we propose Cognitive Decompiler Refinement with Robustness (CoDe-R), a lightweight two-stage code refinement framework. The first stage introduces Semantic Cognitive Enhancement (SCE), a Rationale-Guided Semantic Injection strategy that trains the model to recover high-level algorithmic intent alongside code. The second stage introduces a Dynamic Dual-Path Fallback (DDPF) mechanism during inference, which adaptively balances semantic recovery and syntactic stability via a hybrid verification strategy. Evaluation on the HumanEval-Decompile benchmark demonstrates that CoDe-R (using a 1.3B backbone) establishes a new State-of-the-Art (SOTA) in the lightweight regime. Notably, it is the first 1.3B model to exceed an Average Re-executability Rate of 50.00%, significantly outperforming the baseline and effectively bridging the gap between efficient models and expert-level performance. Our code is available at https://github.com/Theaoi/CoDe-R.
title_zh: CoDe-R：通过理由引导与自适应推理优化反编译器输出的大型语言模型方法
abstract_zh: 二进制反编译是一项关键的逆向工程任务，旨在从剥离符号信息的可执行文件中重构高级源代码。尽管大型语言模型（LLMs）近期展现出巨大潜力，但由于编译过程导致的语义不可逆丢失，这些模型常出现“逻辑幻觉”和“语义错位”问题，生成的代码往往无法正确重执行。在本研究中，我们提出了一种轻量级两阶段代码优化框架——稳健性认知反编译增强（CoDe-R）。第一阶段引入了语义认知增强（SCE），采用基于推理引导的语义注入策略，训练模型同时恢复高层次的算法意图与代码结构；第二阶段在推理过程中引入动态双路径回退（DDPF）机制，通过混合验证策略自适应地平衡语义恢复与语法稳定性。在HumanEval-Decompile基准上的评估表明，使用13亿参数骨干模型的CoDe-R在轻量级模型领域达到了新的最先进水平（SOTA）。尤为突出的是，它是首个在1.3B规模下平均重执行率超过50.00%的模型，显著优于基线方法，有效缩小了高效模型与专家级性能之间的差距。我们的代码已开源，地址为：https://github.com/Theaoi/CoDe-R。

title: Intelligent Algorithm Transplant and Verification System
authors: Zhening Dong, Qing Luo, Dongyue Yang, Yang Liu
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: With the continuous emergence of unmanned platform equipment types and exponential growth in number, the demand for intelligent upgrading of unmanned aerial vehicles, rapid iterative development of intelligent unmanned platforms, tactical combat simulation deduction, etc. is increasing day by day. However, traditional waterfall development system process is lengthy and lacks integrated rapid verification means. Therefore, there is an urgent need to build a new “design-simulation-implementation-test-verification” integrated unmanned platform development system to effectively improve the development iteration speed of unmanned platforms. The intelligent algorithm transplantation and verification systemimplemented in this paper constructsalgorithm analysis component (algorithm interface tool), algorithm function development component, algorithm basic operation tool, algorithm interface conversion development support tool, intelligent algorithm data excitation equipment, intelligent algorithm transplantation running result analysis equipment, and realizes the transplantation of intelligent algorithms in airborne embedded intelligent computing environment.
title_zh: 智能算法移植与验证系统
abstract_zh: 随着无人平台装备类型不断涌现、数量呈指数级增长，对无人机智能化升级、智能无人平台的快速迭代开发、战术作战仿真推演等方面的需求日益迫切。然而，传统的瀑布式开发系统流程冗长，缺乏集成化的快速验证手段。因此，亟需构建一种全新的“设计-仿真-实现-测试-验证”一体化无人平台开发体系，以有效提升无人平台的开发迭代速度。本文所实施的智能算法移植与验证系统，构建了算法分析组件（算法接口工具）、算法功能开发组件、算法基础操作工具、算法接口转换开发支持工具、智能算法数据激励设备、智能算法移植运行结果分析设备等，实现了在机载嵌入式智能计算环境中的智能算法移植。
